metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: The security-invariants AC requires 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot,' but the plan never tasks anyone with capturing the pre-cutover snapshot before the label is deployed, nor where it is stored. Without this artifact existing before cutover, the diff check cannot be performed.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Security-invariants artifact bullet: 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: In the label-deployment AC, prepend: 'Before pushing the label, save `curl .../projects/rebar/access` output to `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` and commit it; ADR-0042 security section diffs the post-deployment response against this file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the ci_failed provenance: either (a) update the parent's split-provenance description to match S3/S4's 'ci_failed derived live from Gerrit' model, or (b) add a ci_failed persistence marker to S3's schema and update S4 AC5 to read it from bot state, choosing one authoritative design.
findings.finding: S3 (0525) directly contradicts the parent's split-provenance model on ci_failed persistence. The parent states ci_failed is 'recorded by the lander into its own state (S3)' alongside needs_rebase. S3's outcome-record section and AC3 say ci_failed is NOT persisted — it is 'derived LIVE from Gerrit at read time.' S4 (73f5) AC5 fallback-precedence implements ci_failed as live-Gerrit-derived (Verified -1), not as a bot-state read, consistent with S3 but inconsistent with the parent.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) — Outcome record section and AC3; parent design section (split-provenance model)
findings.suggested_fix: Update the parent's design text to read: 'the outcome the bot decides — needs_rebase — is recorded by the lander into its own state (S3); ci_failed is derived live from the native Verified -1 vote in Gerrit (not persisted), consistent with S3 AC3 and S4 AC5.' This removes the contradiction without changing S3/S4's behavior.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to S1 (843b): 'Before pushing the Autosubmit label to refs/meta/config, run GET /a/projects/rebar/access and save the response to infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json; commit this file in the SAME Gerrit change that adds the label to project.config.'
findings.finding: The pre-cutover access baseline artifact (saving GET /a/projects/rebar/access to infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json and committing it in the same change that adds the Autosubmit label) is not present as an explicit acceptance-criterion line in any child's AC. The parent explicitly assigns capture to S1 ('S1 owns the capture'), but S1 (843b)'s five ACs contain no step to perform or commit this snapshot. No other sibling covers it.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC section; also child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) — AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S1 AC list: '[ ] Pre-cutover access snapshot: before applying the label to refs/meta/config, capture GET /a/projects/rebar/access → infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json and include it in the same submitted change, so the ADR-0042 access-diff invariant check has a committed baseline.' Also add to S6 AC3: 'The Security invariants section includes a GET /a/projects/rebar/access diff vs infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json, proving the ACL change is exactly label-Autosubmit on refs/heads/* for Contributors/Administrators and nothing else.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S6 AC3 an explicit requirement: 'The ADR-0042 Security invariants section includes the concrete access-diff check: a diff of GET /a/projects/rebar/access against infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json proving the ACL change is exactly label-Autosubmit on refs/heads/* for Contributors/Administrators and nothing else (CVE-2025-1568 / GerriScary posture).'
findings.finding: The parent's security-invariants AC requires the ADR-0042 Security invariants section to include 'a GET /a/projects/rebar/access diff vs the committed infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json baseline' as a concrete check. S6 (b825) AC3, which specifies ADR-0042's required content, does not enumerate this access-diff check. Combined with S1 not capturing the baseline file (Finding 1), this parent criterion is partially uncovered in S6's AC.
findings.location: child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) — AC3 (ADR-0042 content requirements)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to S6 AC3: 'Additionally, the Security invariants section enumerates the three concrete checks from the parent AC: (1) grep infra/gerrit/project.config for function = NoBlock and absence of submit-requirement; (2) diff GET /a/projects/rebar/access vs infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json (baseline committed by S1); (3) a test or code-review assertion that the bot re-verifies is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit.'

findings.finding: The parent's Phase 1 AC8 (Security-invariants artifact) requires 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' as a concrete per-invariant check. S1's AC (Security-invariants) specifies a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config` and a test asserting re-verify logic, and label verification via `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` — but does NOT specify the access diff check against a pre-cutover snapshot. No other sibling covers this specific check.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC5 (Security-invariants artifact); parent Phase 1 AC8
findings.suggested_fix: In S1 AC5 (Security-invariants artifact), add: 'Capture and record a diff of GET /projects/rebar/access before and after the cutover, confirming the only new line is label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors on refs/heads/* and no broader grant was introduced.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ordering ambiguity: either (a) move S1 AC5 into S6's AC list (where ADR-0042 is authored and can be verified), with S1 AC5 rephrased as 'the project.config grep inputs required for the ADR-0042 security invariants check are present in the committed config,' or (b) add S6 as an explicit dependency of S1's completion gate.
findings.finding: S1 (843b) AC5 ('Security-invariants artifact') requires the ADR-0042 Security invariants section to exist and contain specific content, but ADR-0042 is authored by S6 (b825) which depends on S1 being complete. S1 cannot satisfy its own AC5 without S6 existing, yet S6 depends on S1 — creating an ordering violation where S1's completion is gated on a downstream deliverable (S6).
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) — AC5 ('Security-invariants artifact: the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records...'); child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) — Dependencies section
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite S1 AC5 as: 'The committed infra/gerrit/project.config contains function = NoBlock and no submit-requirement referencing Autosubmit — providing the grep-verifiable inputs for the ADR-0042 Security invariants section (authored by S6).' Move the requirement for the full ADR-0042 Security invariants section content to S6 AC3 explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the failure-handling AC with the split-provenance model: explicitly state that CI-fail triggers BOTH removal of Autosubmit AND a bot-state write of the ci_failed outcome (which land then reads), so neither sentence contradicts the other.
findings.finding: The failure-handling AC says 'CI-fail → remove Autosubmit, rely on the native Verified -1 + run logs,' which can be read as ci_failed being derivable purely from native Gerrit state — contradicting the authoritative split-provenance model in Context, which explicitly says ci_failed is 'recorded by the lander into its own state (S3) and read back by land' (not derived live from Gerrit). An implementer following the failure-handling AC could omit the S3 write for ci_failed, breaking the land command's read path.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — Failure handling bullet vs. Context 'Outcome provenance' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the failure-handling bullet to: 'CI-fail → remove `Autosubmit`, write `ci_failed` outcome to bot state (read by `land`), and rely on the native `Verified -1` + run logs for human visibility; rebase-conflict (`409`) → remove `Autosubmit` and write `needs_rebase` to bot state.'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the two S9 residual references from S3's Outcome record section: change 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache' to 'S4 (land command) consumes this cache,' and change 'shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9' to 'shared key across S2/S3/S4.'
findings.finding: S3 (0525) contains two residual references to archived story S9 (batch failure), which no longer exists in the sibling roster. S3's Outcome record section names S9 as a consumer of the cache: 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome.' The parent explicitly states 'S7-S9 archived.'
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) — Outcome record section ('S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache'); also the stack_id description ('shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9')
findings.suggested_fix: In S3 Outcome record: replace 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome' with 'S4 (land command) consumes this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome.' Replace 'shared key across S2/S3/S4/S9' with 'shared key across S2/S3/S4.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability claim that `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` preserves commit-message trailers: add a probe task (manual or scripted) that performs a server-side rebase with this flag on a test change and asserts both `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers are byte-identical in the resulting patchset commit message.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` 'preserve[s] author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers' as a known Gerrit behavior, but this is an unverified capability claim — no spike, test fixture, or external reference in the plan or codebase validates that this flag actually preserves commit-message trailers (vs. merely preserving author identity in the Git author field).
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: '`RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete API probe for `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (a curl with a known test change, or a reference to the instance's own REST API docs page confirming the path), and record the result as a verification artifact in the story implementing chain rebase (S2a/S2b).
findings.finding: The plan's AC asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` as the chain-rebase endpoint, confirmed only by ticket-comment self-attestation ('Verified against the Gerrit REST API docs'). No independent probe, --help output, live API curl, or test fixture in the repo corroborates this path. If the path differs (e.g. requires a plugin, has a different URL form, or was added in a Gerrit version later than the deployed 3.14.1), the chain-rebase leg of the lander silently fails or 404s, forcing fallback to per-member sequential rebases.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1 → Serial auto-lander service AC bullet: `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`
findings.suggested_fix: In story S2a (or S2b), add an AC that runs `curl -X POST .../rebase:chain` against the live instance with a scratch change and records the HTTP response (200 or 409-no-conflict, not 404) before the chain-rebase path is committed. Reference the instance's `Documentation/rest-api-changes.html#rebase-chain` URL as the spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the S5b smoke-test AC (or add an epic-level AC) to explicitly assert that `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers survive a bot-issued `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase — via a `git log --format=%B` check on the post-rebase patchset, not just an uploader-identity check.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author identity, DCO `Signed-off-by` trailers, AND `rebar-ticket` trailers through the bot-issued rebase. This is a specific behavioral claim about Gerrit's rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader feature. No probe, smoke test, or verification fixture validates that all three trailer types survive a bot-triggered rebase. If Gerrit's rebase rewrites the commit message or the bot's HTTP password lacks the `Forge Author` capability, trailers are silently dropped, breaking DCO enforcement and ticket linkage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1 → Serial auto-lander service AC: `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S3 (0525-6d71-8969-4512) as a formal build-order dependency in S5's Dependencies section, not merely a 'Uses' relationship: 'Depends on S3 for the failure/edge branches that are integral to the loop service being containerized.'
findings.finding: S5 (3c55) declares a dependency only on S2 ('Depends on S2 — the core loop this story packages and deploys') and a 'Uses S3' relationship, but does not formally depend on S3. S3's failure handling (CI-fail auto-recheck, 409 conflict path, idempotent Autosubmit removal, rechecking sub-state) is integral to the loop service that S5 containerizes and deploys. If S5 proceeds before S3 is complete, the deployed container will be missing the failure branches.
findings.location: child 3c55-2af3-0c9c-4b11 (S5) — Dependencies section ('Depends on S2... Uses S3's outcome marker for drain/restart reconciliation')
findings.suggested_fix: Update S5 Dependencies to: 'Depends on S2 (perovskite-frilly-grebe, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) and S3 (darksome-starry-sable, 0525-6d71-8969-4512) — S3 adds the CI-fail and rebase-conflict branches to S2's loop; the containerized service is only complete when both S2 and S3 are implemented. Uses S3's outcome marker for drain/restart reconciliation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default poll interval and max-wait ceiling for `land --wait` and the service's 'await Verified +1' loop (e.g. 'poll every 30 s, max 90 min matching the GHA job timeout').
findings.finding: The `land --wait` blocking variant and the 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' service loop have no stated polling interval or maximum wait timeout. An implementing agent must choose these values without guidance, creating an unbounded-wait footgun in CI and a defaultable design gap.
findings.location: AC: 'Agent landing contract (the lead story)' — '--wait' variant; AC: 'Serial auto-lander service' — 'awaits a fresh Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Agent landing contract' AC: 'poll interval: 30 s; max-wait: configurable, default 90 min (matching the GHA workflow timeout); on timeout, surface ci_failed with a timeout sub-reason.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename 'its own state (S3)' to avoid confusion with AWS S3 and state the concrete storage backend (e.g. 'a JSON file in a mounted state dir co-located with the compose service').
findings.finding: The bot's outcome state storage medium is described inconsistently: the Context section calls it 'its own state (S3)' (a label that looks like AWS S3 to a reader skimming), while the Greenfield note calls it 'state dir' (implying a filesystem directory). An implementing agent must decide the actual persistence backend without a stated default.
findings.location: Context — 'Outcome provenance' paragraph ('recorded by the lander into its own state (S3)'); Greenfield note ('the autolander service + its wipChain and state dir')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(S3)' label with '(bot-state-file)' and add a one-line storage spec: 'persisted as a JSON file at $STATE_DIR/outcomes.json, a directory-mounted volume in the compose service.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe the 'Redundant CI runs eliminated' success criterion as a structural/design property verifiable in-session (e.g. 'the serial lander serializes all submissions through a single wipChain worker, provably preventing concurrent rebases — verified by code review of the single-instance design') or explicitly note it is validated by design-inspection rather than empirical measurement.
findings.finding: The success criterion 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated' cannot be verified in-session via repo artifacts or a deterministic command — it requires observing multiple real parallel agent sessions post-deployment and measuring actual CI run counts, making it a post-deployment empirical metric rather than an in-session-completable criterion.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the SC: '(validated structurally: the single-instance wipChain serializes all submissions; no runtime traffic measurement required for acceptance).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define or expand the '(S3)' and '(S4)' parentheticals in the provenance model — if they are story-number references, spell them out (e.g., '(written by Story 3 / the lander service)' and '(read by Story 4 / the land command)'); if they refer to a storage tier, name it explicitly (e.g., 'local state directory on the bot container').
findings.finding: The parentheticals '(S3)' and '(S4)' in the outcome-provenance model are undefined and ambiguous: they could be story-number references (Story 3, Story 4 of the epic) or references to AWS S3 storage plus an unnamed fourth entity. The Greenfield note uses 'state dir' (not S3) for bot persistence, making the architectural choice unclear. This is load-bearing because local-state-dir vs AWS S3 is a deployment/ops decision.
findings.location: Context — 'Outcome provenance (the deliberate split model)' paragraph; Greenfield note
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(S3)' with '(written by the lander service, Story S3)' and '(S4)' with '(read by the land command, Story S4)', or use 'bot-state-dir' consistently with the Greenfield note's 'state dir' terminology.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC criterion (or extend the agent-landing-contract criterion) requiring the outcome-record schema to be defined and committed (e.g., as a JSON schema or typed dataclass in the repo) before or alongside the lander service story, so the lander-write and land-read sides share a versioned contract.
findings.finding: The Greenfield note lists 'outcome-record schema' as a new artifact created by this epic, but no AC criterion explicitly requires the schema to be defined, documented, or tested. The agent landing contract AC covers the land command's external behavior but is silent on what the schema looks like, its fields, or where it lives — leaving the format of the bot↔land read interface unspecified.
findings.location: Greenfield note ('the outcome-record schema … are all NEW artifacts'); AC Phase 1 — Agent landing contract criterion

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the `refs/meta/config` push (e.g., 'revert by pushing the pre-autolander `project.config` snapshot; verify label votes are purged via `DELETE /changes/.../votes/Autosubmit`') and commit it alongside S1's snapshot AC.
findings.finding: The plan mandates pushing the new `Autosubmit` label to `refs/meta/config` (an irreversible, privileged Gerrit operation) but provides no rollback/back-out plan if the label configuration is incorrect (wrong ACL, wrong copyCondition, wrong gating status). Reverting requires a second force-push or config change, and any votes cast under a misconfigured label cannot be retroactively invalidated.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — `Autosubmit` Gerrit label bullet; also S1 `843b` story reference
findings.suggested_fix: In the S1 AC (or ADR-0042 Security invariants), add: 'Rollback: restore `project.config` to its pre-autolander state by pushing the committed snapshot; remove any outstanding `Autosubmit` votes via the Gerrit REST API before the next landing attempt. The committed `access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` serves as the rollback baseline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit cutover sequence to the Docs AC: specify that CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md are rewritten ONLY after the lander service is deployed and the `Autosubmit` label is live, OR add a transitional note in the docs during the intermediate state so in-flight agents are not stranded.
findings.finding: The plan calls for a 'rewrite' of CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md (the landing section) with no cutover/transition plan for agent sessions that are mid-flight under the old manual-landing workflow at the moment of the doc change. Agents following the old instructions (manual rebase-and-retry) will be operating on stale contracts once the docs are rewritten.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — Docs bullet: 'rewrite this repo's landing section (CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a kill-switch / disable mechanism to the lander service spec (e.g., an environment variable `LANDER_ENABLED=false` that causes the bot to skip all queue processing and log a warning, or a documented `docker compose stop autolander` runbook step) and reference it in the failure-handling AC.
findings.finding: The plan describes no kill-switch or disable mechanism for the lander service itself. The failure-handling AC covers per-stack outcomes (remove `Autosubmit`, record outcome) but not the scenario where the bot itself must be urgently stopped (e.g., it is submitting changes incorrectly, or the is:submittable re-verify logic has a bug). Without a documented disable path, an operator facing a misbehaving lander has no plan-specified recovery action.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — 'Failure handling' bullet; 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly require that ADR-0042 (or a companion schema file under `infra/`) documents the S3 outcome-record schema (field names, types, allowed values for the outcome enum) and the `wipChain` state structure, so the `land` command's deserialization has a versioned contract.
findings.finding: The S3 outcome-record schema and `wipChain` state structure are new operational artifacts that the `land` command reads to compose typed outcomes, but no documentation artifact (schema file, ADR section, or runbook entry) is explicitly mandated for them. If the schema is undocumented, future changes to the bot's state format will have no contract to diff against.
findings.location: Context — 'Outcome provenance' paragraph; AC Phase 1 — 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet ('wipChain')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ADR-0042 AC: 'Include a **State schema** section defining the S3 outcome-record JSON shape (fields: `stack_id`, `outcome: needs_rebase | ci_failed`, `recorded_at`, `change_ids[]`) and the `wipChain` lock structure, versioned as `schema_version: 1`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or story note) requiring the await-Verified timeout and polling interval to be exposed as configurable lander settings (e.g., env var or config file), and specify the fallback behaviour (remove `Autosubmit`, record `ci_failed`) when the timeout fires.
findings.finding: The await-fresh-Verified polling loop inside the serial lander will inevitably require a timeout (CI can hang indefinitely), but the plan introduces no configurable threshold for this — it will be hardcoded or absent at implementation time, making it hard to tune as CI characteristics change.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' (serial auto-lander service bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: In the serial lander AC bullet, add: 'await-Verified polling uses a configurable timeout (default: e.g., 2× median CI duration, set via `LANDER_VERIFIED_TIMEOUT_S` env var); on timeout, treat as `ci_failed` (remove `Autosubmit`, record outcome in S3) so the wipChain queue is never blocked by a silent CI failure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a note in ADR-0042) that defines the observable proof for 'redundant CI runs eliminated' — e.g., a logged counter showing at-most-one Verified await per change/stack per lander cycle, or a before/after comparison of GHA run counts for a representative stack.
findings.finding: The success criterion 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated' is a non-deferred, quantitative goal, but no Acceptance Criterion provides an end-state-observable proof that this elimination actually occurs — no baseline measurement, no post-cutover metric, and no test that asserts at-most-one CI run per change/stack processed by the serial lander.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated'
findings.suggested_fix: In ADR-0042's 'Security invariants' or a new 'Operational invariants' section, add: 'The lander MUST log one and only one CI-await event per change/stack attempt; a counter assertion in the lander's integration test validates this.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the 'await fresh Verified +1' step must poll **all N changes in the chain** (not just the front/root change) before attempting submit — for a relation chain, `rebase:chain` mints N patchsets each requiring a fresh Verified +1 under the NO_CODE_CHANGE-only copyCondition.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' in singular form for the rebase→verify→submit loop, but for a relation chain of N changes, `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` mints N new patchsets and each independently loses its Verified vote (strict NO_CODE_CHANGE copyCondition, TRIVIAL_REBASE excluded — ADR-0041/ADR-0040 deliberately unchanged). CI runs per-patchset via gerrit-to-platform dispatch, so there are N separate Verified votes to await. An implementer reading the singular 'a fresh Verified +1' could poll only the front (or root) change, then attempt submit — which would fail because inner chain members don't satisfy the Verified submit-requirement and aren't submittable under FFO. The mechanism only works if the lander waits for Verified +1 on ALL N changes in the chain before submitting.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria → Phase 1 → serial auto-lander service AC bullet: 'rebases to the current tip … → awaits a fresh Verified +1 → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC bullet, change 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' to 'awaits a fresh Verified +1 on **every change in the chain** (all N patchsets minted by rebase:chain must satisfy the submit-requirement before the atomic submit is attempted)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan section (or ADR-0042 background) explicitly noting why Gerrit's native relation-chain submit + `submitWholeTopic` and the GerritForge SubmitQueue plugin are insufficient for the auto-lander use-case (e.g., need for pre-submit rebase-to-tip + Verified re-run + typed outcome reporting), so the custom-service novelty is justified.
findings.finding: The plan builds a custom serial auto-lander service but the repo already has `submitWholeTopic = true` (gerrit.config line 104) enabling native Gerrit chain-submit; Gerrit 3.x also has relation-chain submission semantics built-in. The plan never explicitly discusses why these native mechanisms are insufficient and a custom service is required — this is a missing prior-art justification for the novel custom-bot architecture.
findings.location: Context / Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet; also infra/compose/gerrit.config line 104 (`submitWholeTopic = true`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context or a new 'Design alternatives' note, state: 'Gerrit native chain-submit (`submitWholeTopic = true`) submits a chain atomically but does NOT rebase-to-tip first and does NOT await a fresh Verified run — so the GerriScary invariant (nothing lands whose tree wasn't CI-tested) cannot be met without a bot that drives the rebase → wait-for-CI → submit cycle. The GerritForge SubmitQueue plugin targets multi-instance deployments and is not packaged for the single-box PoC. Hence a custom serial lander is the narrowest-scope fit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before or as part of S1/S2, add a concrete probe story task: call `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with the planned `RebaseInput` body and `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (or the compose stack), record the exact field names and URL paths that work, and pin them in the implementation spec.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` and `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` as the core lander mechanism, but no probe, spike, or codebase usage confirms these specific endpoint paths and field names exist on the deployed Gerrit 3.14.1 instance — this is a new REST-API surface with zero existing usage in the repo.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'rebases to the current tip (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a dedicated SSM SecureString slot (e.g. `/rebar/prod/gerrit-autolander-token`) in `ssm.tf`, a `fetch-secrets.sh` mapping, and confirm the existing `/rebar/prod/*` IAM prefix grant covers it — OR explicitly justify reusing `gerrit-bot-token` with a scope analysis.
findings.finding: The auto-lander service makes authenticated Gerrit REST API calls (POST /changes/{id}/rebase and submit) but the plan specifies no new SSM SecureString slot for its Gerrit bot credential, no fetch-secrets.sh mapping, and no new inline IAM policy — it is silent on whether the lander reuses the existing `gerrit-bot-token` slot (which is already scoped to the review-bot) or needs a dedicated credential.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — 'Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"/rebar/prod/gerrit-autolander-token"` to `local.rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` (following the same placeholder+`ignore_changes` pattern as `gerrit-bot-token`) and add a `fetch-secrets.sh` mapping entry. The existing `/rebar/prod/*` SSM read grant in `iam.tf` already covers this path, so no new IAM policy is needed — document this explicitly in an `iam_autolander.tf` anchor file following the `iam_s2.tf` / `iam_s4a.tf` pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `monitoring_autolander.tf` (or equivalent section) with a CloudWatch alarm for lander service health (e.g. a restart-loop / error-marker counter wired to the existing SNS alerts topic), matching the pattern of `monitoring_s4b.tf` and `monitoring_1fa8.tf`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the auto-lander service logs to 'container stdout → existing CloudWatch monitoring' but does not provision a CloudWatch alarm for lander health — unlike every other service on the box (review-bot has a voter_errors alarm in monitoring_s4b.tf, g2p has a g2p_dispatch_errors alarm in monitoring_1fa8.tf, autodeploy has a deploy_errors alarm). A crashed or looping lander would be undetected.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — Observability bullet: 'the bot logs to its own service logs (container stdout → existing CloudWatch monitoring), NOT session_log'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the secret-management strategy for the lander bot's Gerrit API token and AWS/S3 credentials in the plan (e.g., Docker compose secrets / AWS Secrets Manager / IAM instance role) so no plaintext secret lives in IaC, image layers, or service logs on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.finding: The lander service's Gerrit API credentials and AWS/S3 credentials cross a trust boundary (outbound network calls to Gerrit REST API; writes to S3), but the plan does not declare how these secrets are provisioned or managed — the sole reference is 'deploys like the review-bot,' which is a pointer, not a declaration. A plaintext secret in a compose env file or baked into the image would be a secret-lifecycle gap on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.location: AC: 'Serial auto-lander service … deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose'; Context: 'recorded by the lander into its own state (S3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: 'Lander bot credentials (Gerrit HTTP password / OAuth token; AWS credentials) are injected via [compose secrets | AWS Secrets Manager | IAM task role] — no plaintext secret in Dockerfile, compose YAML, or container stdout logs.' For AWS, prefer an IAM instance/task role (no static key at all) scoped to the single state-S3 bucket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the S3 state bucket's access posture in the plan: private / account-scoped, IAM policy scoped to the lander bot role (write) and the principals running `land` (read), server-side encryption enabled — and note this in the Security-invariants section of ADR-0042.
findings.finding: The S3 state bucket used by the lander bot (outcome records for `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed`) crosses a trust boundary — the bot writes it, the `land` command reads it — but the plan declares no access posture (IAM bucket policy, public vs. private, who may read/write). An undeclared posture on a boundary-crossing data path is itself the gap under the T5c rubric.
findings.location: Context / Agent contract section: 'the two outcomes the bot decides … are recorded by the lander into its own state (S3) and read back by land'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a least-privilege declaration for the lander bot's Gerrit account to the plan (and to the Security-invariants section of ADR-0042): name the Gerrit group the bot belongs to, enumerate only the permissions it needs (Rebase on behalf of uploader, Submit on refs/heads/main for project rebar), and confirm no broader Admin/Verified-cast permissions are granted.
findings.finding: The lander bot must hold elevated Gerrit permissions — at minimum 'Rebase on behalf of other users' and 'Submit' on the target ref — but the plan does not declare the bot account's Gerrit group membership or permission set. For a component that can submit to `main` (the FFO-protected branch), the least-privilege dimension requires an explicit, verifiable permission declaration.
findings.location: AC Phase 1: 'Serial auto-lander service … rebases to the current tip (POST /changes/{id}/rebase … RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true) … ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: 'The lander bot's Gerrit service account is a member of a dedicated `Autolander` group granted only: `Submit` on `refs/heads/main` for project `rebar`, and `Rebase on behalf of other users` — no Code-Review, Verified, or Admin capabilities. This is verifiable via `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diff in ADR-0042.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the outcome-record schema (minimum fields: change/stack id, outcome enum value, timestamp, optional detail string) in the epic AC or a named `docs/land-contract.md` stub, so both the bot's S3 writer and the `land` command's S3 reader have a shared contract.
findings.finding: The 'outcome-record schema' is named as a greenfield artifact the epic creates (Greenfield note) and is the mechanism by which `land` reads `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` from bot state (S3), but the plan never defines its field vocabulary — what keys does a stored outcome record have (e.g., change-id, outcome string, timestamp, error detail)?
findings.location: Greenfield note + AC: Agent landing contract + Context: Outcome provenance
findings.suggested_fix: Add a minimal schema block to the AC (or reference docs/land-contract.md): `{ "stack_id": "<gerrit-change-id>", "outcome": "needs_rebase|ci_failed", "recorded_at": "<ISO-8601>", "detail": "<optional free text>" }`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an exit-code table to the 'Agent landing contract' AC item mapping each outcome enum value (and at minimum `pending`/`error`/`not_requested` states) to its distinct integer exit code.
findings.finding: The AC promises the `land` command returns 'ONE typed terminal outcome … + distinct exit code' but never tabulates the exit-code-to-outcome mapping. An agent acting on the command's result must know which exit code corresponds to which outcome.
findings.location: AC: Agent landing contract — 'distinct exit code'
findings.suggested_fix: Example table: merged=0, needs_rebase=1, ci_failed=2, review_failed=3, pending=75 (EX_TEMPFAIL), error=7, not_requested=4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a precedence/conflict-resolution rule to the split-provenance model: e.g., 'MERGED (live Gerrit) always wins over any S3 marker; review_failed takes precedence over ci_failed when both votes are present; S3 markers are invalidated if the change is no longer in an active Autosubmit state.'
findings.finding: The `land` command's outcome-composition decision rule is underspecified: the plan says it composes the outcome from S3 bot-state reads and S4 live Gerrit reads, but provides no precedence rule for the case where multiple conditions apply simultaneously (e.g., S3 has a `needs_rebase` marker AND live Gerrit shows `LLM-Review -1`, or S3 has `ci_failed` AND change status is MERGED).
findings.location: Context: Outcome provenance (split-provenance model) + AC: Agent landing contract

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a timeout and its fallback outcome to the epic AC: specify a maximum await duration for `Verified +1` in the lander loop (e.g., 'if no fresh Verified within N minutes, remove Autosubmit and record ci_failed') and a `--wait` timeout for the `land` command (e.g., 'if outcome not terminal within M minutes, exit with a `pending`/`timeout` outcome and non-zero code').
findings.finding: The 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' step in the serial lander and the `land --wait` blocking variant have no timeout specified in the epic AC; the fallback when CI never completes (infinite hang) is undefined at the epic level.
findings.location: AC: Serial auto-lander service — 'awaits a fresh Verified +1'; AC: Agent landing contract — 'blocking (--wait)'

findings.finding: The session log's `relates_to -> 547b-4aca-0076-4a36` link is entirely absent from the plan — no mention of ticket 547b-4aca-0076-4a36 anywhere in the epic body, context, AC, or out-of-scope sections.

The plan's AC text asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader` as the Gerrit REST API field for rebase impersonation, but the correct field name in the Gerrit REST API is `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`. The remediation comment trail explicitly identifies and corrects this ("the RebaseInput field is `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`, not `on_behalf_of_uploader`"), but the correction was never propagated back into the epic plan body, leaving implementers who read the plan — not the comment thread — with the wrong parameter name.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: '`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `on_behalf_of_uploader` with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` in the AC bullet: '`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader: true` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
